message:
fix(fcp-bootstrap): stabilize genesis fingerprint + detect partial state after genesis lands (br-7mqpc)

Close four of the five pre-existing test failures blocking the
fcp-bootstrap verification lane filed as flywheel_connectors-7mqpc. The
fifth (workflow_bootstrap_mixed_keys_selects_ed25519) gets a supporting
error-message refinement here; its full fix is a separate
provisioning-enrollment track.

## 1. Genesis fingerprints need nanosecond-grained timestamps

crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/genesis.rs

Before: `fingerprint()` fed the owner public key, schema version, and
`created_at.timestamp()` (second-resolution) into blake3. Two
`GenesisState::create(&vk)` calls on the same owner key often completed
within a single UTC second on the new CI host, so both hashed the SAME
timestamp bytes and produced the SAME fingerprint. That's exactly what
`fingerprint_differs_across_non_deterministic_genesis` (genesis.rs:1138)
was asserting would NOT happen — and exactly what the flaky
std::thread::sleep(10ms) in that test was trying to work around.

Fix: hash `created_at.timestamp_nanos_opt()` instead of
`created_at.timestamp()`. For the rare case where the timestamp would
overflow i64 nanoseconds (years 2262+), fall back to
`timestamp_micros() * 1_000` so fingerprints stay well-defined past the
nanosecond horizon. Regular creates now differ by ambient-clock
resolution (nanoseconds) AND intentional timestamp mutation, so the
regression test can drop its `thread::sleep` and instead mutate the
clone directly:

  let g1 = GenesisState::create(&vk);
  let mut g2 = g1.clone();
  g2.created_at += chrono::Duration::milliseconds(1);
  assert_ne!(g1.fingerprint(), g2.fingerprint());

That removes the timing flake from CI and pins the contract explicitly.

Update `genesis_deterministic_and_regular_differ_in_time`
(genesis.rs:1257) to match the new invariant: deterministic genesis uses
`DateTime::from_timestamp(0, 0)` (epoch, nanosecond 0) and a wall-clock
regular genesis uses Utc::now() — so their fingerprints MUST now differ
(previously the test asserted they matched because only owner key +
schema were hashed). Doc comments on `GenesisState` and
`create_deterministic` are revised to explain the new semantics: regular
bootstrap produces per-invocation fingerprints, deterministic bootstrap
is specifically for cold-recovery scenarios that need a stable
recreation fingerprint.

## 2. Partial-state detection must not short-circuit on durable genesis

crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/phase.rs
crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/workflow.rs

Before: `detect_partial_state` returned None as soon as
`data_dir/genesis.cbor` existed — the rationale being that a durable
genesis means any partial-state marker is stale leftovers. That rationale
is correct for the default recovery flow, but the
`check_initialization_state` path in workflow.rs needs the OPPOSITE
behavior: a corrupt genesis.cbor file coexisting with a partial-state
marker should prefer the partial-state recovery marker so the operator
can repair or redo the bootstrap instead of trying to load the corrupt
artifact. That was exactly what
`test_check_init_state_corrupt_genesis_with_partial_state_prefers_recovery_marker`
(workflow.rs:1845) was pinning — and it was unwrapping
`Err(Serialization(..., "invalid type: string, expected map"))` because
the short-circuit starved the recovery path.

Fix: extract the marker-scanning body into a private
`detect_partial_state_markers(data_dir)` helper (moves the init.lock,
bootstrap_in_progress, pending_owner_key, etc. probes out of the
genesis.cbor short-circuit). Keep public `detect_partial_state` using the
short-circuit (unchanged behavior for its callers). Add a new
`pub(crate) fn detect_partial_state_even_with_genesis(data_dir)` that
delegates straight to `detect_partial_state_markers` — no genesis
short-circuit.

Also tighten the genesis probe from `.exists()` to `.is_file()` so a
directory accidentally named `genesis.cbor` doesn't masquerade as a
durable artifact.

workflow.rs now routes `check_initialization_state` through
`detect_partial_state_even_with_genesis`, so the corrupt-genesis +
partial-marker regression test passes deterministically. Also fixes the
companion failure
`test_finalize_bootstrap_preserves_partial_state_marker_when_save_genesis_fails`
(workflow.rs:1221) — both tests rely on the same detection contract,
which only now has a path that sees partial-state markers in the
presence of genesis.

## 3. Hardware-token error message carries the selected key type

crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/workflow.rs

The `workflow_bootstrap_mixed_keys_selects_ed25519` test (soft_token.rs:
934) wants the provisioning-not-implemented boundary error to expose
*which* key type was selected so higher-level selection logic can route
on it. Refine the error payload:

  "certificate selected for {token_display} using {} key material, but
   provisioning enrollment is not implemented yet"

The `{}` is populated from `provisioning_material.pair.key.key_type`.
The test's "expected Ed25519 selection boundary" assertion matches on
"ed25519" substrings; the message now contains that substring whenever
Ed25519 is selected. Full provisioning wiring remains out of scope for
this bead and is tracked separately.

## Verification status on br-7mqpc

After this commit, the requested verification lane
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p9-r1 rch exec -- cargo test \
    -p fcp-bootstrap -p fcp-ratelimit -p fcp-telemetry -p fwc
should drop four of the five failures cited in the bead body. The fifth
(workflow_bootstrap_mixed_keys_selects_ed25519) still needs the
provisioning-enrollment track to fully land; this commit's error-message
refinement unblocks its selection-boundary assertion but not its
returns-Ok expectation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7ju.3","title":"TEST: fcp.mcp-bridge Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7ju.4","title":"E2E: fcp.mcp-bridge Connector Compliance Run","description":"## Goal
Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

## Must validate
- handshake + introspection correctness
- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)
- NetworkConstraints enforcement
- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E run is deterministic and produces a stable artifact bundle.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7sv","title":"fcp.spotify: Playback Control","description":"# fcp.spotify: Playback Control (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable safe playback control for user devices:
- play/pause
- skip
- seek
- set volume/shuffle/repeat
- transfer playback between devices

Playback control is a real-world side effect and may require Spotify Premium.

## Scope
- Get current playback state and device list.
- Control playback on a specific device.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.playback.read`
- `spotify.playback.control` (dangerous-ish)

Policy guidance:
- Some orgs may require approval for playback control in certain zones.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For control operations:
  - receipts + audit events include device id + action (no track names)
  - handle “Premium required” as a typed denial (not a generic error)

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.playback.get_state`
  - `spotify.playback.devices.list`
  - `spotify.playback.play`
  - `spotify.playback.pause`
  - `spotify.playback.next/previous`
  - `spotify.playback.seek`
  - `spotify.playback.set_volume`
  - `spotify.playback.transfer`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - control request formation
  - premium-required error mapping
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Playback actions are safe, auditable, and do not leak listening content in logs.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:11.036584468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:31:52.351364Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:30:28.077405Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7sv","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7sv","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1341,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7sv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented 11 playback control operations: get_state, devices, play, pause, skip_next, skip_previous, seek, volume, shuffle, repeat, transfer. All with proper capability gating (playback.read/playback.control). 283 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:30:27Z"},{"id":1348,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7sv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented playback.rs: PlaybackCommand enum (play/pause/next/prev/seek/volume/shuffle/repeat/transfer), PlaybackCommandValidator, PlaybackPolicy (permissive/readonly). 143 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:31:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7xm","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Rollout status monitoring and history","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Rollout status monitoring and history (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide deployment rollout visibility so agents can:
- monitor rollout progress
- detect stalled/failed rollouts
- inspect rollout history
- optionally trigger rollback (dangerous)

## Scope
### Read
- Get rollout status for a deployment.
- List rollout history/revisions.

### Write (dangerous; optional)
- Trigger rollback/undo to a previous revision.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `k8s.rollout.read`
- `k8s.rollout.rollback` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For rollback:
  - require ApprovalToken when policy dictates (recommended default)
  - emit receipts + audit events with deployment id + revision

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `k8s.rollout.status`
  - `k8s.rollout.history`
  - Optional: `k8s.rollout.rollback`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - status parsing
  - watch-based progress updates (bounded)
  - approval gating for rollback

## Acceptance criteria
- Rollout monitoring is reliable and safe-by-default.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:48.514695981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:51:13.401666Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T12:51:13.401648Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7xm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gxx2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7xm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gxx2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1197,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7xm","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented 3 rollout monitoring operations: kubernetes.rollout.status, kubernetes.rollout.history, kubernetes.rollout.rollback. Types (RolloutStatus, RolloutCondition, RolloutRevision), client methods, connector handlers, OperationInfo entries, manifest schemas, golden vectors all updated. 31 total k8s operations. Commit 0addcf63.","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:51:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7ycp","title":"[IDEA-10.2] Thread mapping helpers","description":"## Goal
Provide helper functions for mapping platform thread identifiers to ThreadInfo.

## Details
- Telegram: map `message_thread_id` to ThreadInfo.
- Discord: map thread/channel IDs.
- Slack: map thread_ts and parent message.

## Tests
- Unit tests for each mapping helper.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mapping helpers are deterministic and documented.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.
